item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A new supplicant for Caesar. Repentance for a heretic's life.

Something of a problem there; pre-Christian Rome didn't really have the concept of a heretic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My love of classical history is biasing me but I love this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well-written and spooky enough to nod at. Also obtuse enough of an idea to immediately garner up-votes, as long as nothing else is terribly wrong (nothing is). Props for the phrases "selectively-corporeal entities" and "stands and addresses an unknown observer".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this sums up my reaction as well. Not knowing anything about Pliny, I didn't have much to latch onto and overall didn't find it too interesting, but it's well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't really go anywhere. Beyond the straightforward ghostly retelling of past events, which can sometimes work as a simple SCP but isn't something I get super excited about 3000 articles in, the only additions are wiki cliches of killing people it touches and people being captured by the scene.

However, I enjoyed the historical setting and detail and felt this was competently executed enough to not downvote.

item.title: SCP-3397: My First SCP
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading my first SCP. I've spent over a month working on it and I hope it entertains you or at least doesn't bore you enough to downvote it.

The image in this article is: Death of Pliny the Elder, Roman Naturalist by Louis Figuier, from Vies des Savants Illustres, 1866.

Thank you to all who critiqued and helped me to refine my SCP!
